data.content:
The file is generated successfully (`obj/Debug/net8.0/Generated/GeneratedInfo.cs`) but never added to the compilation. The `GenerateSampleCode` target writes the file but doesn't include it in the `Compile` item group, so the C# compiler never sees it.

**Fix:** Add the generated file to `<Compile>` inside the target.

arguments.new_str:
<!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>

    <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates the `.cs` file before `CoreCompile`, but it doesn't add the generated file to the `Compile` item group. The MSBuild `Compile` items are collected before the target runs, so the generated file is never passed to the C# compiler.

The fix is to add an `<ItemGroup>` inside the target that includes the generated file in the `Compile` items:

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
</ItemGroup>
```

This needs to be added after the `WriteCodeFile` task call in the `GenerateSampleCode` target.

arguments.new_str:
<!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>

    <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

data.content:
Build succeeds and runs correctly.

**Root cause:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote `GeneratedInfo.cs` to the intermediate output directory but never added it to the `<Compile>` item group. MSBuild collects `Compile` items before custom targets run, so the compiler never saw the generated file.

**Fix:** Added `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />` inside the target, after the file is written. This tells the C# compiler to include the generated source file during compilation.
